# Source Card: Treatment of newly diagnosed severe aplastic anemia in children: Evidence-based recommendations

## Summary
This is a peer-reviewed, expert-panel evidence-based recommendations paper (Shimano et al., Pediatr Blood Cancer 2024;71(8):e31070; PMID 38757488) on managing children with newly diagnosed severe aplastic anemia (SAA). For the pediatric CDS module its relevance is at the scope-exit / referral boundary: SAA is a rare, potentially fatal marrow-failure diagnosis typically reached from a pancytopenic CBC, and management is a hematology specialty domain — not a lab reference-interval to encode. The paper's diagnostic-severity thresholds (modified Camitta criteria) are referenced by the panel but were not accessible in the retrievable text and are recorded below as GAPs rather than asserted.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) SAA is a rare, potentially fatal disorder with excellent treated outcomes but variable, adult-vs-pediatric-divergent management — locator: Abstract, sentences 1-2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:98ebf499b4dd9d9d5fe9e4e602a2a36ad937533e597788bd8b142579da4f25be]"
- (ev_002) Some treatment aspects remain under active clinical-trial investigation — locator: Abstract, sentence 3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7bda7d394da98ad1e6f781ca2b2adab5e43230bff9cbe44a8a8a8986066a6663]"
- (ev_003) Disease rarity means limited clinician experience, motivating the consolidated guidance — locator: Abstract, sentence 4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b702b58a670e4a5f052824589191652b64c2ffd79b848b54837258c2a8697268]"
- (ev_004) The recommendations are an up-to-date evidence-based approach for newly diagnosed pediatric SAA — locator: Abstract, sentence 5 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:2b8e6f563b0272f2a6538816e96c8699ef04628b886c4801b484241e884ec865]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children (pediatric hematology) with newly diagnosed severe aplastic anemia; sex not differentiated; not a neonatal (GA/PNA-indexed) or reference-interval source.
- Assay/method dependence: not applicable — this source carries no laboratory reference intervals or analyzer-method-dependent numeric cutoffs in the accessible text.
- Lifecycle: published 2024-05 (Pediatr Blood Cancer 2024;71(8):e31070); no retire date; represents current (as of 2024) evidence-based pediatric SAA treatment guidance. A companion paper covers relapsed/refractory pediatric SAA (doi:10.1002/pbc.31075).

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes — scope-exit / referral relevance. Severe aplastic anemia is a red-flag hematologic diagnosis (pancytopenia + marrow hypoplasia) that sits outside routine primary-care CDS: its confirmation and management (HSCT vs immunosuppressive therapy) belong to pediatric hematology. For the CDS converter this source supports a referral/scope-boundary concept ("suspected marrow failure / SAA -> pediatric hematology"), NOT any diagnostic, dosing, or transfusion directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (paywall): Full text is behind the Wiley paywall and the article is not in PubMed Central; only the abstract was retrievable verbatim (PubMed, PMID 38757488). All extracted points derive from the abstract.
- GAP (threshold not locatable): The numeric diagnostic criteria for severe / very severe aplastic anemia (modified Camitta criteria — e.g., bone-marrow cellularity, absolute neutrophil count, platelet count, and reticulocyte count cutoffs; and vSAA ANC cutoff) are referenced by this panel but were NOT locatable in the accessible text of source 10.1002/pbc.31070. Per contract these numbers are therefore NOT asserted here; they must be sourced from a passage carrying a verbatim quote (e.g., the paper's Introduction/Definitions section on full-text access, or a primary Camitta-criteria source).
- GAP (treatment numeric not locatable): Reported first-line outcome figures (e.g., matched-sibling-donor HSCT cure rate) circulate in secondary summaries of this work but no verbatim passage from the source itself was accessible; not asserted.
- Directive boundary: This card records disease-scope and referral context only. Per the pediatric-CDS contract, NO diagnosis, treatment, dosing, or transfusion directive is emitted; the card is a proposal for the CDS converter, not an executable rule.
- No conflicts recorded with other cards at this time (the source is a specialty-management guideline, not a lab reference-interval standard, so it does not overlap CBC reference-interval sources).
